title: Hydra daily digest config
description: Config for the daily status digest. Subscribed via './hydra connect digest <channel>'. Emitted via scripts/build-daily-digest.sh. Scheduled by the Scheduled autopickup tick (step 1.6). Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-daily-digest.md (ticket #144).

enabled.description: Master switch. When false, the autopickup tick's digest check (step 1.6) no-ops. Re-running './hydra connect digest' re-prompts for this.

channel.description: Where the paragraph goes. 'stdout' writes to memory/digests/YYYY-MM-DD.md AND stdout (no external transport needed). 'slack' / 'webhook' / 'email' append to logs/digest-out.jsonl in Phase 1; actual transport is a follow-up ticket consuming that JSONL.

time.description: Local HH:MM at or after which the digest fires on a given day. Default 09:00. Uses host local timezone — same semantic as the Monday retro check (see docs/specs/2026-04-17-scheduled-retro.md).

destination.description: Channel-specific target. stdout: path prefix (default 'memory/digests/'). slack: channel ID (falls back to state/connectors/slack.json:default_channel if empty). webhook: env-var NAME holding the URL (never paste the URL itself — tokens stay out of this file). email: address string.

last_digest_run.description: ISO-8601 local timestamp of the most recent digest emission. Used by the autopickup tick's idempotency check so the same day's 09:00 window doesn't double-fire. Null = never run.

max_chars.description: Override for the 500-char cap. Rarely used; exists for operators who pipe to channels with different size constraints.

full_status_pointer.description: Trailing pointer that replaces the default './hydra status' suffix when truncation occurs. Operators on non-CLI channels may prefer a URL here. Defaults to './hydra status' in the composer.
